item.description: A brief overview of IP addressing, with a focus on CIDR notation and an introduction to ipcalc.

item.description: A case study illustrating Terraform techniques for expressing moderately complex business logic.

item.description: Collect all pages of data from a paginated HTTP API in JavaScript using async/await.

item.title: Hopscotch Coffee Illustration
item.description: 18" x 24" micron pen drawing for Hopscotch Coffee’s Bloomington, IN shop.

item.title: All CSS Modal Dialogs
item.description: A simple example demonstrating all-CSS, no-JavaScript modal dialogs.

item.title: JavaScript Modules
item.description: A few notes on JavaScript modules.

item.title: TimesGrapher
item.description:
TimesGrapher seeks to shed light on what topics are covered by the New York Times from year-to-year. The simple application leverages the New York Times API to visualize the differences in coverage offered to any two topics in any year between 1981 and 2013, in effect providing insight on the year-to-year relevancy of current events, as well as arguable editorial bias.

The application is built with Rails and Backbone.js.

TimesGrapher was built as a hobby project; it is not affiliated with the New York Times.

View TimesGrapher »

View source on Github »

item.title: Rails API Integration Tests
item.description: A simple pattern for authoring integration tests for a Rails app.

item.title: A Simple Ruby Class Example
item.description: A basic Ruby class example to illustrate a few of the language's features.

item.title: Automating Product Availability Alerts
item.description: Too much internet. Automate your web browsing.

item.title: Automated Tests: What, Why, How?
item.description: An overview of automated tests for the uninitiated.

item.title: wp2middleman
item.description:
wp2middleman is a Ruby gem that migrates the Wordpress posts in a Wordpress XML export file to middleman-style markdown files.

wp2middleman provides a wp2mm commandline executable, as well as a WP2Middleman module.

View source and documentation on Github »

item.title: 31 Dream Homes
item.description: For Artclash’s 2012 West Philadelphia Fun-a-Day show, I drew 31 dream homes, one each day through the month of January.

item.title: Lobber
item.description:
Lobber is a simple command line utility used to quickly upload a directory to Amazon S3.

Lobber is built with Ruby and can be downloaded from Rubygems.

View its source code on GitHub »

item.title: Philadelphia Police Advisory Commission Complaint Map
item.description:
My map of 2009 – 2012 complaints filed with the Philadelphia Police Advisory Commission won first place at the 2013 Barcamp News Innovation Hackathon.

The map plots the details surrounding complaints filed with the Police Advisory Commission by citizens against Philadelphia Police Officers. Its data is published by Open Data Philly and has been imported to a public Google Spreadsheet. According to Open Data Philly, the data comes directly from the Police Advisory Commission Complaint Database.

The map was built in an afternoon and uses Leaflet, Node.js, and Backbone. Its source code is available on Github.

View the map »

item.title: Nicar 2013 Conference
item.description: A few NICAR 2013 highlights and reflections.

item.description: How to use Tabletop.js as your JavaScript app's datastore.

item.title: TEDxPhilly
item.description: The organizers of TEDxPhilly contacted me to create a seam-less, infinitely tiling illustration to be used as a background image throughout the conference’s web collateral. The illustration is type-based and inspired by “the city,” the 2011 conference theme.

item.title: Basic Command Line Tools
item.description: Some beginner's notes; collected for a recently-hired junior developer.

item.title: How to Contribute to a Project on GitHub
item.description: A simple how-to outlining the Github fork, branch, and PR workflow.

item.title: CSS3: An All-CSS, Image-less Play Button
item.description: I wondered if it could be done; here's a quick and dirty technique.

item.title: CSS3: Using Transform to Make a Cube
item.description: My quick solution to a CSS challenge.

item.title: How to Make Testable Private Methods in JavaScript
item.description: A controversial technique for writing unit tests against private methods.

item.title: JavaScript Design Patterns
item.description: My notes on Addy Osmani's JavaScript design patterns ebook.

item.title: Simplified Find/Replace from the Command Line
item.description: An overview of a quick bash function I recently wrote.

item.title: How to Exclude Files from SVN Commits
item.description: A bash function for working around a Subversion annoyance.

item.title: Basic Overview of the Surround Plugin for Vim
item.description: My notes on using Vim's surround plugin.

item.title: Our Philadelphia
item.description:
I partnered with Gabriel Farrell and Cecily Anderson to design and build ourphiladelphia.org for Common Cause, a nonprofit, nonpartisan citizen’s lobbying organization working to promote open, honest, and accountable government.

Our Philadelphia is a community website designed to provide information to Philadelphians about the role of money in local politics. The site features an address-based representative search tool, a news publishing platform, RSS feeds, user accounts and profiles, as well as an extensive database of local campaign donor information. The project was made possible by the Samuel S. Fels Fund.

I was responsible for logo design and branding, user interface design, information architecture, and front end development. The site is built on Django.

item.title: Quiet Logger: HTML5 WordPress Theme
item.description: An overview of a basic Wordpress theme I was tasked in creating.

item.title: Hometown Typeface
item.description: A typeface I created in illustrator.

item.title: OurPhiladelphia.org Launched
item.description: Some colleagues and I recently released ourphiladelphia.org.

item.title: Battles
item.description: Battles: Sea and Land are two illustration-based narrative zines made in the Summer of 2006.

item.title: Brainworms
item.description: T-shirt design.

item.title: Clap Yr Hands
item.description: Clap Yr Hands is a zine I occasionally publish, usually featuring illustration and collage.

item.title: free103point9
item.description:
Gabe Farrell and I developed this site for the New York-based transmission arts advocacy organization free103point9.

The site is built on Django. In addition to brochuring free103point9, the site also serves as a transmission arts digital archive, hosts an event calendar, and houses free103′s online radio station.

item.title: Taylor & Juliette
item.description: I created a simple brochure website and letter-pressed invite for Taylor & Juliette’s wedding. The website and invite feature original illustrations, as well as a hand-drawn typeface created for the wedding materials.

item.title: Anthroapology
item.description: This quarter-size zine is a collection of illustrations I made while living and teaching English in Bangkok, Thailand in 2005/2006.

item.title: Polaroid Celluloid
item.description: Hand drawn illustration for the zine Polaroid Celluloid.

item.title: 31 Portraits
item.description: For Artclash’s 2010 Fun-a-Day show, I drew 31 portraits, one for each day of the month of January. The illustrations were compiled in Anthrotopography, a small, photocopied zine.

item.title: 31 Patterns
item.description: Nate Johnson, Kara Schlindwein, and I collaborated on this book of 31 illustrated patterns for Artclash’s annual Fun-A-Day art show. We created one pattern each day throughout the month of January 2009 and compiled our finished work in a handmade book. The patterns are designed to tile infinitely.

item.title: Parcell Press
item.description: I designed and developed the logo, identity, and ecommerce website for Parcell Press, an independent zine, art, and book distributor.

item.title: Muscle Crap
item.description: A few of my illustrations were featured in Muscle Crap, a limited edition artists’ book published by Salt Tooth Press in 2004.
